message:
fix(h2): cap stream pruning threshold and amortize active stream counting

Two related improvements to H2 connection stream management:

1. connection.rs — Pruning threshold cap:
   When max_concurrent_streams is set to u32::MAX (the default unlimited
   value), the saturating_mul(2) pruning threshold effectively disabled
   pruning entirely, allowing unbounded memory growth on long-lived
   connections. Now caps the base value at 16,384 before doubling, so
   pruning triggers at 32,768 tracked streams regardless of the limit.

2. stream.rs — Amortized active stream counting:
   Previously, allocate_stream_id() performed an O(N) filter+count on
   every call to check active streams against max_concurrent_streams.
   Now defers the O(N) scan until total tracked streams reach the limit,
   and combines counting with retain() to prune closed streams in the
   same pass. This eliminates redundant scans when stream count is well
   below the limit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
